title: Associative Property of Multiplication
essential_question: How can you use the Associative Property to multiply?
strategies: Change the grouping of factors — product stays the same
Group factors to make easier multiplication

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162

pretest.question: Does (2 × 3) × 4 = 2 × (3 × 4)?
choices: B. No, first is 24, second is 20

pretest.question: Which property says changing the grouping doesn't change the product?
pretest.explanation: The Associative Property is about grouping.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Associative Property
learn.content: When multiplying three numbers, you can change which two you multiply first. (2×3)×5 = 2×(3×5). Both = 30. The grouping changes, but the product doesn't.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Using Parentheses
learn.content: Parentheses tell you what to do first. (4×2)×3: do 4×2=8 first, then 8×3=24. 4×(2×3): do 2×3=6 first, then 4×6=24. Same answer, different path.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Making Multiplication Easier
learn.content: Group factors to make a ×10 fact. Example: 5 × 2 × 4. Group (5×2) first to get 10, then 10×4 = 40. Much easier than 5×8 = 40!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Associative vs. Commutative
learn.content: Commutative changes the ORDER: 3×4 = 4×3. Associative changes the GROUPING: (3×4)×5 = 3×(4×5). Different properties, same big idea — the product stays the same.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Look for Friendly Pairs
learn.content: Before you compute, scan the three factors for a pair that makes 10 or a ×5. Example: 2 × 7 × 5 — group the (2×5) to get 10, then 10×7 = 70.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 결합법칙 — 괄호 위치를 바꿔도 곱은 같다
learn.content: 세 수를 곱할 때, 어디부터 곱해도 결과는 같음 — 결합법칙. 예: (2 × 3) × 5 = 6 × 5 = 30. 예: 2 × (3 × 5) = 2 × 15 = 30. 두 가지 모두 같은 답 30. 괄호의 위치는 결과를 바꾸지 않음. 주의: 결합법칙은 인자의 '순서'가 아니라 '그루핑(괄호)'을 바꾸는 것 (순서 변경은 교환법칙).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 친한 짝(Friendly Pair) 먼저 곱하기 — 빠른 풀이
learn.content: 결합법칙의 진짜 힘: 쉬운 짝부터 골라 먼저 곱하기. 친한 짝: ×2, ×5, ×10 (덧셈에서 '10 만들기'와 같은 원리). 예: 7 × 2 × 5. 어렵게: (7 × 2) × 5 = 14 × 5 = 70. 쉽게: 7 × (2 × 5) = 7 × 10 = 70 ⭐. 팁: '×2와 ×5가 만나면 묶어라!' (= ×10이 되어 즉답).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 결합법칙 활용 전략 요약
learn.content: 전략 1 ×2와 ×5를 보면 묶어 ×10 만들기.   예: 6 × 5 × 2 → 6 × (5 × 2) = 6 × 10 = 60. 전략 2 같은 수가 두 번 있으면 묶기 (제곱).   예: 3 × 3 × 4 → (3 × 3) × 4 = 9 × 4 = 36. 전략 3 ×1이 있으면 무시 (곱셈 항등원).   예: 5 × 1 × 7 = 5 × 7 = 35. 전략 4 작은 수끼리 먼저 (큰 곱은 마지막). 검증: 어떻게 묶어도 결과는 같음 — 가장 쉬운 길 선택!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.6 'Associative Property of Multiplication' pp.159-162
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive and Associative Properties
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Which grouping makes 4 × 2 × 5 easiest?
try.explanation: 4 × 10 = 40 is easier than 8 × 5 (even though both give 40).

feedback.incorrect: 2 × 5 = 10, so grouping that way gives 4 × 10 = 40, which is easiest.

try.question: Fill in: (4 × 3) × 2 = 4 × (3 × ?). What goes in the blank to make both sides equal?
try.explanation: The Associative Property only regroups the SAME factors, so the blank is 2. Both sides = 24.
hints: The Associative Property doesn't change the factors.
The three factors are still 4, 3, and 2.

feedback.incorrect: Same three factors: 4, 3, and 2. The blank must be 2.

practice_r1.question: Which equation shows the Associative Property?
practice_r1.explanation: Only B changes the grouping of the same factors. A is Commutative, C is Distributive, D is Identity.

feedback.incorrect: B: same factors, just regrouped with parentheses.

practice_r2.question: Fill in: (a × b) × c = a × (b × ?). What goes in the blank?
practice_r2.explanation: The Associative Property keeps the same three factors — just regrouped. The blank is c.
hints: The factors on both sides must match.
Left side has a, b, c. Right side has a, b, ?.

feedback.incorrect: The factors a, b, c appear on both sides. Blank = c.
